hub_subtitle: Inglês para o trabalho — do aperto de mão ao acordo fechado, em 7 aulas

lesson.menu_desc: Read a real e-mail from an international buyer -- find the request and the deadline, and answer in the past simple -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 4 -- Reading a Business E-mail
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ziláudio | Lesson 4 -- Reading a Business E-mail

listenings.text: Hi Zilaudio, this is David from Continental Grain in Chicago. I just sent you an e-mail, so this is only a quick message. In the e-mail, I attached a short form. I asked for two things: your total tons and your final price. Please read the e-mail and reply to me by Friday, because our schedule this month is very tight. After you send the form, I will call you to confirm everything. Thank you, and talk to you soon.

Good morning, and here is a short reminder about your week. You have two deadlines. The first one is the soybean form for Continental Grain. Please send it before Friday. The second one is the price confirmation for the meeting on Monday. Remember to check every attachment before you reply, and confirm the dates in your schedule. If you send the documents early, you have more time before the weekend. Have a good week.

paras: Thank you for our call last week. I sent you our price last Monday, and I received your first numbers on Wednesday. My team asked me for two more documents before we sign the order.

In this e-mail, I attached a short form. Please fill it in and send it back to me. I also have one request: I need your answer by Friday. The deadline is important this week, because our schedule is very tight.

Can you confirm the total tons and the final price? After you reply, I will call you to close the order. Please check the attachment and write to me before the weekend. Best regards, David Miller -- Continental Grain, Chicago.
reading.source: E-mail received by Ziláudio for the English for Business & Agribusiness package

reading.prompt: What is the best title for this e-mail?
choices: A Buyer Asks for Documents and a Reply by Friday
How to Drive from Chicago to Sorriso

items: David sent his price last Monday.
"I sent you our price last Monday."
David needs Zilaudio's answer by Monday.
"I need your answer by Friday" -- not Monday.
David attached a short form to the e-mail.
"In this e-mail, I attached a short form."
David will call Zilaudio before he replies.
"After you reply, I will call you" -- the call comes after the reply.

bank.label: How to reply to a business e-mail
items: I received your message and the attachment.
I will send the form back by Friday.
I confirm the total tons and the final price.
Can you send me the new schedule?

items.situation: The buyer asks you to confirm the price. Start your reply.
tips: I confirm the total tons and the final price.
confirm = say clearly that it is correct.

items.situation: He set a deadline: Friday. Tell him you will answer in time.
tips: I will send you my answer by Friday.
by + the day of the deadline.

items.situation: He attached a form to the e-mail. Tell him you got it.
tips: I received your e-mail and the attachment.
attachment = the file that comes with the e-mail.

items.situation: You need his new plan of dates for the meeting. Ask for it.
tips: Can you send me the new schedule?
schedule = the plan of times and dates.

items.situation: Close your e-mail in a polite, professional way.
tips: regards = a polite word before your name.

items.situation: Tell him what you did: you sent the documents yesterday.
tips: sent = past simple of send, a completed action.
